title: Joint Modeling of Networks and Attributes with iglm
text: vignette provides introduction iglm package, designed estimating joint probability models incorporate network structures. package allows users analyze individual attributes network connections jointly influence outcomes interest. can create iglm object specifying network structure attributes interest. simple example:
code: n_actor <- 100  attribute_info <- rnorm(n_actor) attribute_cov <- diag(attribute_info) edge_cov <- outer(attribute_info, attribute_info, FUN = function(x, y) {   abs(x - y) }) set.seed(123)  alpha <- 0.3 block <- matrix(nrow = 50, ncol = 50, data = 1) neighborhood <- as.matrix(Matrix::bdiag(replicate(n_actor / 50, block, simplify = FALSE)))  overlapping_degree <- 0.5 neighborhood <- matrix(nrow = n_actor, ncol = n_actor, data = 0) block <- matrix(nrow = 5, ncol = 5, data = 0) size_neighborhood <- 5 size_overlap <- ceiling(size_neighborhood * overlapping_degree)  end <- floor((n_actor - size_neighborhood) / size_overlap) for (i in 0:end) {   neighborhood[(1 + size_overlap * i):(size_neighborhood + size_overlap * i), (1 + size_overlap * i):(size_neighborhood + size_overlap * i)] <- 1 } neighborhood[(n_actor - size_neighborhood + 1):(n_actor), (n_actor - size_neighborhood + 1):(n_actor)] <- 1  type_x <- "binomial" type_y <- "binomial" object <- iglm.data(neighborhood = neighborhood, directed = F, type_x = type_x, type_y = type_y, n_actor = n_actor)

title: Joint Modeling of Networks and Attributes with iglm
text: can specify model formula includes various network statistics attribute effects. example: fully define model, need set sampler MCMC estimation set necessary parameters:
code: formula <- object ~ edges + attribute_y + attribute_x + degrees # Parameters of edges(mode = "local"), attribute_y, and attribute_x gt_coef <- c(3, -1, -1) # Parameters for degree effect gt_coef_degrees <- c(rnorm(n = n_actor, -2, 1)) # Define the sampler sampler_tmp <- sampler.iglm(   n_burn_in = 100, n_simulation = 10,   sampler_x = sampler.net.attr(n_proposals = n_actor * 10),   sampler_y = sampler.net.attr(n_proposals = n_actor * 10),   sampler_z = sampler.net.attr(n_proposals = sum(neighborhood > 0) * 10),   init_empty = F )  model_tmp_new <- iglm(   formula = formula,   coef = gt_coef, coef_degrees = gt_coef_degrees, sampler = sampler_tmp,   control = control.iglm(accelerated = F, max_it = 200, display_progress = F) )

title: Joint Modeling of Networks and Attributes with iglm
text: specified model, can simulate new data based fitted parameters:
code: # Simulate new networks model_tmp_new$simulate() # Get the samples tmp <- model_tmp_new$get_samples()

title: Joint Modeling of Networks and Attributes with iglm
text: can estimate model parameters using estimate method:
code: # First set the first simulated network as the target for estimation model_tmp_new$set_target(tmp[[1]]) model_tmp_new$estimate() model_tmp_new$iglm.data$degree_distribution(plot = TRUE)

title: Joint Modeling of Networks and Attributes with iglm
text: estimation, can assess model fit using various diagnostics:
code: model_tmp_new$assess(formula = ~ degree_distribution +   geodesic_distances_distribution + edgewise_shared_partner_distribution + mcmc_diagnostics) model_tmp_new$results$plot(model_assessment = T)

text: Create list control parameters `iglm` estimation algorithm.

title: Set Control Parameters for iglm Estimation — control.iglm
code: control.iglm(   estimate_model = TRUE,   display_progress = FALSE,   return_samples = TRUE,   offset_nonoverlap = 0,   var_method = "Mean-value",   non_stop = FALSE,   tol = 0.001,   max_it = 100,   return_x = FALSE,   return_y = FALSE,   return_z = FALSE,   accelerated = TRUE,   exact = TRUE )

title: Set Control Parameters for iglm Estimation — control.iglm
text: estimate_model (logical) `TRUE` (default), main model parameters estimated. `FALSE`, estimation skipped preprocessing done. display_progress (logical) `TRUE`, display progress messages progress bar estimation. Default `FALSE`. return_samples (logical). TRUE (default), return simulated network/attribute samples (.e., iglm.data objects) generated estimation (applicable). offset_nonoverlap (numeric) value added linear predictor dyads 'overlap' set. Default `0`. var_method (string) Method variance estimation. Options "Mean-value" (default), "Godambe", "Hessian". mean-value version described Section 3.3 Fritz et al. (2025), Godambe method described Schmid Hunter (2023), "Hessian" option just assumes pseudo likelihood correct likelihood. non_stop (logical) `TRUE`, estimation algorithm continues `max_it` iterations, ignoring `tol` convergence criterion. Default `FALSE`. tol (numeric) tolerance level convergence. estimation stops change coefficients iterations less `tol`. Default `0.001`. max_it (integer) maximum number iterations estimation algorithm. Default `100`. return_x (logical). TRUE, return change statistics x attribute Default FALSE. samples. Default `FALSE`. (Note: `return_samples=TRUE` likely implies ). return_y (logical). TRUE, return change statistics y attribute Default FALSE. return_z (logical). TRUE, return change statistics z network. Default FALSE. accelerated (logical) `TRUE` (default), accelerated MM algorithm used based Quasi Newton scheme described Supplemental Material Fritz et al (2025). exact (logical) `TRUE`, pseudo Fisher information calculated exact assessing uncertainty estimates. Default `FALSE`.

title: Copenhagen Network Study — copenhagen
Copenhagen Network Study — copenhagen
text: iglm.data provides following information: z_network \(E \times 2\) integer matrix representing     undirected friendship network ($Z$). x_attribute logical/binomial vector length \(N\) indicating     gender (1 female, 0 male). y_attribute numeric vector length \(N\) representing     log-transformed total call duration minutes:     \(y_i = \log(\frac{\text{seconds}}{60})\). neighborhood matrix defining proximity-based constraint     space. Pairs included cumulative     physical proximity exceeded 24 hours observation period. fix_x Boolean TRUE, indicating \(x\) attribute     treated exogenous.

title: Copenhagen Network Study — copenhagen
text: following preprocessing steps carried : Temporal Aggregation: Proximity data (Bluetooth scans)     aggregated sessions. session break defined     temporal gap exceeding 10 minutes. Recursive Pruning: recursive filter removed actors missing     gender information isolated actors either     friendship (z_network) proximity (neighborhood) networks,

what: Model Specification for iglm Terms — iglm-terms
title: Model Specification for iglm Terms — iglm-terms
text:
help pages iglm describe model details model fitting estimation. Generally, model specified via sufficient statistics, can decomposed two parts: \(\mathbf{g}_i(x_i^*,y_i^*) = \mathbf{g}_i(x_i,y_i)= (g_i(x_i,y_i))\): vector unit-level functions ("g-terms")     describe relationship individual actor \(\)'s     predictors (\(x_i\)) response (\(y_i\)). \(\mathbf{h}_{,j}(x_i^*,x_j^*, y_i^*, y_j^*, z) = \mathbf{h}_{,j}(x,y,z)= (h_{,j}(x,y,z))\): vector pair-level functions ("h-terms")     specify connections (\(z\)) responses (\(y_i, y_j\))     pair units \(\{,j\}\) depend wider     network structure. term defines component model's features, sum unit-level components, \(\sum_i g_i(x_i,y_i)\), /pair-level components, \(\sum_{
e j} h_{,j}(x,y,z)\). implemented terms grouped three categories: Attribute Terms: Depend individual attributes \(x_i\) \(y_i\). Network Terms: Depend connections \(z_{,j}\). Joint Attribute/Network Terms: Depend individual attributes connections. degrees: Degrees: Specifies node-level fixed effects. Estimation requires MM algorithm constraint. edges(mode = "global"): Edges: Captures baseline propensity tie formation \(z_{,j}\), partitioned structural boundary \(c_{,j}\). global: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = z_{,j}\) local: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = c_{,j} z_{,j}\) alocal: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = (1 - c_{,j}) z_{,j}\) mutual(mode = "global"): Mutual Reciprocity: Evaluates reciprocal tie formation directed networks. global: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = z_{,j} z_{j,}\) (\(< j\)) local: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = c_{,j} z_{,j} z_{j,}\) (\(< j\)) alocal: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = (1 - c_{,j}) z_{,j} z_{j,}\) (\(< j\)) cov_z(data, mode = "global"): Dyadic Covariate: Exogenous dyadic covariate \(w_{,j}\) influence edge formation. global: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = w_{,j} z_{,j}\) local: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = c_{,j} w_{,j} z_{,j}\) alocal: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = (1 - c_{,j}) w_{,j} z_{,j}\) cov_z_out(data, mode = "global"): Covariate Sender: Exogenous monadic covariate \(v_{}\) influence generating outgoing tie. global: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = v_i z_{,j}\) local: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = c_{,j} v_i z_{,j}\) alocal: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = (1 - c_{,j}) v_i z_{,j}\) cov_z_in(data, mode = "global"): Covariate Receiver: Exogenous monadic covariate \(v_{j}\) influence receiving incoming tie. global: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = v_j z_{,j}\) local: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = c_{,j} v_j z_{,j}\) alocal: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = (1 - c_{,j}) v_j z_{,j}\) cov_x(data = v): Nodal Covariate (X): Effect unit-level exogenous covariate \(v_i\) endogenous attribute \(x_i\). \(g_i(x_i,y_i) = v_i x_i\) cov_y(data = v): Nodal Covariate (Y): Effect unit-level exogenous covariate \(v_i\) endogenous attribute \(y_i\). \(g_i(x_i,y_i) = v_i y_i\) attribute_xy(mode = "global"): Nodal Attribute Interaction (X-Y): Interaction attributes \(x_i\) \(y_i\). global: \(g_i(x_i,y_i) = x_i y_i\) local: \(g_i(x_i,y_i) = x_i \sum_{j \mathcal{N}_i} y_j + y_i \sum_{j \mathcal{N}_i} x_j\) alocal: \(g_i(x_i,y_i) = x_i \sum_{j 
otin \mathcal{N}_i} y_j + y_i \sum_{j 
otin \mathcal{N}_i} x_j\) attribute_yz(mode = "local"): Attribute Sum (Y-Z): Models additive effect \(y_i\) \(y_j\) edge formation within local neighborhoods. attribute_xz(mode = "local"): Attribute Sum (X-Z): Models additive effect \(x_i\) \(x_j\) edge formation within local neighborhoods. inedges_y(mode = "global"): Attribute -Degree (Y-Z): Influence endogenous \(y_j\) -degree reception. global: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = y_j z_{,j}\) local: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = c_{,j} y_j z_{,j}\) alocal: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = (1 - c_{,j}) y_j z_{,j}\) outedges_y(mode = "global"): Attribute -Degree (Y-Z): Influence endogenous \(y_i\) -degree formation. global: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = y_i z_{,j}\) local: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = c_{,j} y_i z_{,j}\) alocal: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = (1 - c_{,j}) y_i z_{,j}\) inedges_x(mode = "global"): Attribute -Degree (X-Z): Influence endogenous \(x_j\) -degree reception. global: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = x_j z_{,j}\) local: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = c_{,j} x_j z_{,j}\) alocal: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = (1 - c_{,j}) x_j z_{,j}\) outedges_x(mode = "global"): Attribute -Degree (X-Z): Influence endogenous \(x_i\) -degree formation. global: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = x_i z_{,j}\) local: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = c_{,j} x_i z_{,j}\) alocal: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = (1 - c_{,j}) x_i z_{,j}\) attribute_x: Attribute (X): Intercept attribute \(x\). \(g_i(x_i,y_i) = x_i\) attribute_y: Attribute (Y): Intercept attribute \(y\). \(g_i(x_i,y_i) = y_i\) edges_x_match(mode = "global"): Attribute Match (X-Z): Models homophily/matching binary attribute \(x\). global: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = \mathbb{}(x_i = x_j) z_{,j}\) local: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = c_{,j} \mathbb{}(x_i = x_j) z_{,j}\) edges_y_match(mode = "global"): Attribute Match (Y-Z): Models homophily/matching binary attribute \(y\). global: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = \mathbb{}(y_i = y_j) z_{,j}\) local: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = c_{,j} \mathbb{}(y_i = y_j) z_{,j}\) spillover_yy_scaled(mode = "global"): Scaled Y-Y-Z Outcome Spillover: Normalizes \(y\)-outcome spillover influence relevant -degree topology. global: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = y_i y_j z_{,j} / 	ext{deg}()\) local: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = c_{,j} y_i y_j z_{,j} / 	ext{deg}(, 	ext{local})\) spillover_xx_scaled(mode = "global"): Scaled X-X-Z Outcome Spillover: Normalizes \(x\)-outcome spillover influence relevant -degree topology. global: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = x_i x_j z_{,j} / 	ext{deg}()\) local: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = c_{,j} x_i x_j z_{,j} / 	ext{deg}(, 	ext{local})\) spillover_yx_scaled(mode = "global"): Scaled Y-X-Z Treatment Spillover: Normalizes cross-attribute \(y_i \x_j\) spillover influence. global: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = y_i x_j z_{,j} / 	ext{deg}()\) local: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = c_{,j} y_i x_j z_{,j} / 	ext{deg}(, 	ext{local})\) spillover_xy_scaled(mode = "global"): Scaled X-Y-Z Treatment Spillover: Normalizes cross-attribute \(x_i \y_j\) spillover influence. global: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = x_i y_j z_{,j} / 	ext{deg}()\) local: \(h_{,j}(x,y,z) = c_{,j} x_i y_j z_{,j} / 	ext{deg}(, 	ext{local})\) gwesp(data, mode = "global", variant = "OSP", decay = 0): Geometrically Weighted Edgewise-Shared Partners: Models triadic closure propensity conditioning existing edges. Types dictate path constraint: OTP, ITP, OSP, ISP directed; symm undirected. gwdsp(data, mode = "global", variant = "OSP", decay = 0): Geometrically Weighted Dyadwise-Shared Partners: Models triadic potential irrespective closing edge. Types dictate path constraint: OTP, ITP, OSP, ISP directed; symm undirected. gwdegree(mode = "global", decay = 0): Geometrically Weighted Degree: Captures degree distribution utilizing exponential decay parameter. gwidegree(mode = "global", decay = 0): Geometrically Weighted -Degree: Captures -degree distribution utilizing exponential decay parameter. gwodegree(mode = "global", decay = 0): Geometrically Weighted -Degree: Captures -degree distribution utilizing exponential decay parameter. spillover_yc_symm(data = v, mode = "local"): Symmetric Y-C-Z Treatment Spillover: Bidirectional mapping exogenous covariate \(v\) endogenous trait \(y\) interaction. spillover_xy(mode = "local"): Directed X-Y-Z Treatment Spillover: Maps cross-attribute \(x_i \y_j\) treatment assignment. spillover_yc(mode = "local"): Directed Y-C-Z Treatment Spillover: Exogenous covariate \(v\) interacting endogenous trait \(y\). spillover_yx(mode = "local"): Directed Y-X-Z Treatment Spillover: Maps cross-attribute \(y_i \x_j\) treatment assignment. spillover_yy(mode = "local"): Symmetric Y-Y-Z Outcome Spillover: Propagates \(y\)-outcome spillover effects. spillover_xx(mode = "local"): Symmetric X-X-Z Outcome Spillover: Propagates \(x\)-outcome spillover effects. transitive: Transitivity (Local): Indicator evaluating presence local transitive triad configuration. nonisolates: Non-Isolates: Captures frequency nodes degree strictly greater zero. isolates: Isolates: Captures frequency nodes degree zero.

what: Constructor for the iglm.data R6 object — iglm.data
title: Constructor for the iglm.data R6 object — iglm.data
text: Creates `iglm.data` object, stores network attribute data. function acts user-friendly interface `iglm.data` R6 class generator. handles data input, infers parameters like number actors (`n_actor`) network directedness (`directed`) explicitly provided, processes network data consistent edgelist format, calculates overlap relation based optional neighborhood definition, performs extensive validation inputs.

title: Constructor for the iglm.data R6 object — iglm.data
code: iglm.data(   x_attribute = NULL,   y_attribute = NULL,   z_network = NULL,   neighborhood = NULL,   directed = TRUE,   n_actor = NA,   type_x = "binomial",   type_y = "binomial",   scale_x = 1,   scale_y = 1,   fix_x = FALSE,   fix_z = FALSE,   fix_z_alocal = FALSE,   return_neighborhood = TRUE,   file = NULL )

title: Constructor for the iglm.data R6 object — iglm.data
text: x_attribute numeric vector first unit-level attribute. y_attribute numeric vector second unit-level attribute. z_network matrix representing network. Can 2-column edgelist square adjacency matrix. neighborhood optional matrix neighborhood representing local dependence. Can 2-column edgelist square adjacency matrix. tie `neighborhood` actor j indicates j neighborhood , implying dependence respective actors. directed logical value indicating `z_network` directed. `NA` (default), directedness inferred symmetry `z_network`. n_actor integer number actors system. `NA` (default), `n_actor` inferred attributes network matrices. type_x Character string type `x_attribute`. Must one `"binomial"`, `"poisson"`, `"normal"`. Default `"binomial"`. type_y Character string type `y_attribute`. Must one `"binomial"`, `"poisson"`, `"normal"`. Default `"binomial"`. scale_x positive numeric value scaling (e.g., variance "normal" type). Default 1. scale_y positive numeric value scaling (e.g., variance "normal" type). Default 1. fix_x (logical) `TRUE`, 'x' predictor held fixed estimation/simulation (fixed design regression). Default `FALSE`. fix_z (logical) `TRUE`, 'z' network held fixed estimation/simulation (fixed network design). Default `FALSE`. Setting TRUE, allows practicioners estimate autologistic actor attribute models, introduced binary settings Daraganova, G., & Robins, G. (2013). fix_z_alocal (logical) `TRUE`, edges outside overlap region fixed, else random (default). return_neighborhood Logical. `TRUE` (default) `neighborhood` `NULL`, full neighborhood (dyads) generated implying global dependence. `FALSE`, neighborhood set. file (character) Optional file path load saved `iglm.data` object state.

what: Networks with Unit-Level Attributes (R6 Class) — iglm.data_generator
title: Networks with Unit-Level Attributes (R6 Class) — iglm.data_generator
text: `iglm.data` class container storing, validating, analyzing unit-level attributes (x_attribute, y_attribute) connections (z_network).

title: Networks with Unit-Level Attributes (R6 Class) — iglm.data_generator
text: x_attribute (`numeric`) vector first unit-level attribute. y_attribute (`numeric`) vector second unit-level attribute. z_network (`matrix`) primary network structure 2-column integer edgelist. neighborhood (`matrix`) Read-. secondary/neighborhood structure 2-column integer edgelist. empty matrix provided. overlap (`matrix`) Read-. calculated overlap relation (dyads shared neighbors `neighborhood`) 2-column integer edgelist. empty matrix overlap computed available. directed (`logical`) Indicates `z_network` treated directed. n_actor (`integer`) total number actors (nodes) network. type_x (`character`) specified distribution type `x_attribute`. type_y (`character`) specified distribution type `y_attribute`. scale_x (`numeric`) scale parameter associated `x_attribute`. scale_y (`numeric`) scale parameter associated `y_attribute`. fix_x (`logical`) Indicates `x_attribute` fixed estimation/simulation. fix_z (`logical`) RIndicates `z_network` fixed estimation/simulation. descriptives (`list`)list storing computed descriptive statistics network attributes. fix_z_alocal (`logical`) Flag indicating whether nonoverlap edges treated random.

title: Networks with Unit-Level Attributes (R6 Class) — iglm.data_generator
text: iglm.data_generator$new() iglm.data_generator$set_z_network() iglm.data_generator$set_type_x() iglm.data_generator$set_type_y() iglm.data_generator$set_scale_x() iglm.data_generator$set_scale_y() iglm.data_generator$set_x_attribute() iglm.data_generator$set_y_attribute() iglm.data_generator$gather() iglm.data_generator$set_fix_z_alocal() iglm.data_generator$delete_isolates() iglm.data_generator$save() iglm.data_generator$set_fix_x() iglm.data_generator$set_fix_z() iglm.data_generator$mean_z() iglm.data_generator$mean_x() iglm.data_generator$mean_y() iglm.data_generator$x_distribution() iglm.data_generator$y_distribution() iglm.data_generator$edgewise_shared_partner() iglm.data_generator$set_neighborhood_overlap() iglm.data_generator$dyadwise_shared_partner() iglm.data_generator$geodesic_distances_distribution() iglm.data_generator$geodesic_distances() iglm.data_generator$edgewise_shared_partner_distribution() iglm.data_generator$dyadwise_shared_partner_distribution() iglm.data_generator$degree_distribution() iglm.data_generator$degree() iglm.data_generator$spillover_degree_distribution() iglm.data_generator$plot() iglm.data_generator$print() iglm.data_generator$clone()

title: Networks with Unit-Level Attributes (R6 Class) — iglm.data_generator
text: Create new `iglm.data` object, includes data two attributes one network.

title: Networks with Unit-Level Attributes (R6 Class) — iglm.data_generator
code: iglm.data_generator$new(   x_attribute = NULL,   y_attribute = NULL,   z_network = NULL,   neighborhood = NULL,   directed = NA,   n_actor = NA,   type_x = "binomial",   type_y = "binomial",   scale_x = 1,   scale_y = 1,   fix_x = FALSE,   fix_z = FALSE,   fix_z_alocal = TRUE,   return_neighborhood = TRUE,   file = NULL )

title: Networks with Unit-Level Attributes (R6 Class) — iglm.data_generator
text: x_attribute numeric vector first unit-level attribute. y_attribute numeric vector second unit-level attribute. z_network matrix representing network. Can 2-column edgelist square adjacency matrix. neighborhood optional matrix neighborhood representing local dependence. Can 2-column edgelist square adjacency matrix. tie `neighborhood` actor j indicates j neighborhood , implying dependence respective actors. directed logical value indicating `z_network` directed. `NA` (default), directedness inferred symmetry `z_network`. n_actor integer number actors system. `NA` (default), `n_actor` inferred attributes network matrices. type_x Character string type `x_attribute`. Must one `"binomial"`, `"poisson"`, `"normal"`. Default `"binomial"`. type_y Character string type `y_attribute`. Must one `"binomial"`, `"poisson"`, `"normal"`. Default `"binomial"`. scale_x positive numeric value scaling (e.g., variance "normal" type). Default 1. scale_y positive numeric value scaling (e.g., variance "normal" type). Default 1. fix_x Logical. `TRUE`, `x_attribute` treated fixed model estimation simulation. Default `FALSE`. fix_z Logical. `TRUE`, `z_network` treated fixed model estimation simulation. Default `FALSE`. fix_z_alocal Logical. `TRUE` (default), alocal dyads neighborhood fixed. return_neighborhood Logical. `TRUE` (default) `neighborhood` `NULL`, full neighborhood (dyads) generated implying global dependence. `FALSE`, neighborhood set. file (character) Optional file path load saved `iglm.data` object state.

what: Construct an iglm Model Specification Object — iglm
title: Construct an iglm Model Specification Object — iglm
text: R package iglm implements generalized linear models (GLMs) studying relationships among attributes connected populations, responses connected units can dependent. extends GLMs independent responses dependent responses can used studying spillover connected populations network-mediated phenomena. based joint probability model dependent responses (\(Y\)) connections \((Z)\) conditional predictors (X).

title: Construct an iglm Model Specification Object — iglm
code: iglm(   formula = NULL,   coef = NULL,   coef_degrees = NULL,   sampler = NULL,   control = NULL,   name = NULL,   file = NULL )

title: Construct an iglm Model Specification Object — iglm
text: formula model `formula` object. left-hand side name `iglm.data` object available calling environment. See iglm-terms details specifying right-hand side terms. coef Optional numeric vector initial coefficients structural (non-degrees) terms `formula`. `NULL`, coefficients initialized zero. Length must match number terms. coef_degrees Optional numeric vector specifying initial degrees coefficients. Required `formula` includes degrees terms, otherwise `NULL`. Length must match `n_actor` (undirected) `2 * n_actor` (directed). sampler object class sampler.iglm, controlling MCMC sampling scheme. `NULL`, default sampler settings used. control object class control.iglm, specifying parameters estimation algorithm. `NULL`, default control settings used. name Optional character string specifying name model. file Optional character string specifying file path load previously saved  iglm.object disk (RDS format). provided, arguments ignored object loaded file.

title: Construct an iglm Model Specification Object — iglm
text: object class iglm.object.

title: Construct an iglm Model Specification Object — iglm
text: joint probability density specified $$f_{\theta}(y,z,x) \propto \Big[\prod_{=1}^{N} a_x(x_i)\, a_y(y_i) \exp(\theta_g^T \mathbf{g}_i(x_i^*, y_i^*)) \Big] \times \Big[\prod_{\ne j} a_z(z_{,j}) \exp(\theta_h^T \mathbf{h}_{,j}(x_i^*,x_j^*, y_i^*, y_j^*, z)) \Big],$$ defined two distinct sets user-specified features: \(\mathbf{g}_i(x_i^*, y_i^*)= (g_i(x_i^*, y_i^*))\): vector unit-level functions ("g-terms")     describe relationship individual actor \(\)'s     predictors (\(x_i\)) response (\(y_i\)). \(\mathbf{h}_{,j}(x_i^*,x_j^*, y_i^*, y_j^*, z)= (h_{,j}(x_i^*,x_j^*, y_i^*, y_j^*, z))\): vector pair-level functions ("h-terms")     specify connections (\(z\)) responses (\(y_i, y_j\))     pair units \(\{,j\}\) depend wider     network structure. separation allows model simultaneously capture individual-level behavior (via \(g_i\)) dyadic, network-based dependencies (via \(h_{,j}\)), including local dependence limited overlapping neighborhoods. help page documents various statistics available 'iglm', corresponding \(g_i\) (attribute-level) \(h_{,j}\) (pair-level) components joint model. See iglm-terms details specifying model terms via formula interface.

title: Construct an iglm Model Specification Object — iglm
text: Fritz, C., Schweinberger, M., Bhadra, S., D.R. Hunter (2025). Regression Framework Studying Relationships among Attributes Network Interference. Journal American Statistical Association, appear. Schweinberger, M. M.S. Handcock (2015). Local Dependence Random Graph Models: Characterization, Properties, Statistical Inference. Journal Royal Statistical Society, Series B (Statistical Methodology), 7, 647-676. Schweinberger, M. J.R. Stewart (2020). Concentration Consistency Results Canonical Curved Exponential-Family Models Random Graphs. Annals Statistics, 48, 374-396. Stewart, J.R. M. Schweinberger (2025). Pseudo-Likelihood-Based M-Estimation Random Graphs Dependent Edges Parameter Vectors Increasing Dimension. Annals Statistics, appear.

title: Construct an iglm Model Specification Object — iglm
code: # Example usage: # Create a iglm.data data object (example) n_actor <- 50 neighborhood <- matrix(1, nrow = n_actor, ncol = n_actor) xyz_obj <- iglm.data(   neighborhood = neighborhood, directed = FALSE,   type_x = "binomial", type_y = "binomial" ) # Define ground truth coefficients gt_coef <- c("edges_local" = 3, "attribute_y" = -1, "attribute_x" = -1) gt_coef_pop <- rnorm(n = n_actor, -2, 1) # Define MCMC sampler sampler_new <- sampler.iglm(   n_burn_in = 100, n_simulation = 10,   sampler_x = sampler.net.attr(n_proposals = n_actor * 10),   sampler_y = sampler.net.attr(n_proposals = n_actor * 10),   sampler_z = sampler.net.attr(n_proposals = sum(neighborhood > 0) * 10),   init_empty = FALSE ) # Create iglm model specification model_tmp_new <- iglm(   formula = xyz_obj ~ edges(mode = "local") +     attribute_y + attribute_x + degrees,   coef = gt_coef,   coef_degrees = gt_coef_pop,   sampler = sampler_new,   control = control.iglm(     accelerated = FALSE,     max_it = 200, display_progress = FALSE   ) ) # Simulate from the model model_tmp_new$simulate() model_tmp_new$set_target(model_tmp_new$get_samples()[[1]])  # Estimate model parameters model_tmp_new$estimate()  # Model Assessment model_tmp_new$assess(formula = ~degree_distribution)  model_tmp_new$results$plot(model_assessment = TRUE)

title: iglm Objects (R6 Class) — iglm.object.generator
text: `iglm.object` class encapsulates components required define, estimate, simulate generalized linear model interference. includes model formula, coefficients, underlying network attribute data (via `iglm.data` object), sampler controls, estimation controls, storage results.

Journal Royal Statistical Society, Series B (Statistical Methodology), 7, 647-676.

title: iglm Objects (R6 Class) — iglm.object.generator

title: iglm Objects (R6 Class) — iglm.object.generator
text: Calculates predicted values nodal covariates (x), outcome variable (y), network structure (z). function supports two prediction modes: marginal (based Monte Carlo integration simulated samples) conditional (based analytical linear predictor point estimates).

title: iglm Objects (R6 Class) — iglm.object.generator
iglm Objects (R6 Class) — iglm.object.generator
text: variant character string specifying type prediction generate. Must one : "marginal": Computes predictions aggregating MCMC samples stored   internal results. samples exist, self$simulate() triggered automatically. "conditional": Computes predictions using systematic component   Generalized Linear Model (GLM). calculates linear predictor \(\eta = X\beta\)   (plus offset degrees terms network) applies inverse link function   \(\mu = g^{-1}(\eta)\). Defaults c("conditional", "marginal"). type character vector indicating components predict. Options : "x": Nodal covariates. "y": Nodal outcome variable. "z": Dyadic network structure (interaction probabilities). Defaults c("x", "y", "z").

title: iglm Objects (R6 Class) — iglm.object.generator
text: Marginal Predictions: variant = "marginal", function approximates expected value via Monte Carlo integration: $$\hat{\mu} = \frac{1}{S} \sum_{s=1}^{S} h^{(s)}$$ \(h^{(s)}\) realized values \(s\)-th simulation sample (either attribute x, y connections z). network z, results marginal edge probability matrix averaged sampled networks. Conditional Predictions: variant = "conditional", function calculates theoretical mean \(\mu\) based estimated coefficients \(\hat{\theta}\): Binomial families: \(\mu = (1 + \exp(-\eta))^{-1}\) (Logistic). Poisson families: \(\mu = \exp(\eta)\) (Exponential). Gaussian families: \(\mu = \eta\) (Identity). network component z, linear predictor includes dyadic covariates, degrees effects (sender/receiver variances), structural offsets.

title: iglm Objects (R6 Class) — iglm.object.generator
text: list containing requested predictions: x, y matrix data frame first column actor ID subsequent   columns represent predicted mean values. z data frame containing edgelist columns: sender, receiver,   prediction (probability intensity). results also invisibly stored internal state private$.results.

what: Constructor for a iglm Sampler — sampler.iglm
title: Constructor for a iglm Sampler — sampler.iglm
text: Creates object class `sampler.iglm` (`R6`) holds parameters controlling MCMC sampling process `iglm` models. includes global settings like number simulations burn-, well references specific samplers network (`z`) attribute (`x`, `y`) components. function provides convenient way specify settings passing `iglm` constructor simulation functions.

title: Constructor for a iglm Sampler — sampler.iglm
code: sampler.iglm(   sampler_x = NULL,   sampler_y = NULL,   sampler_z = NULL,   n_simulation = 100,   n_burn_in = 10,   init_empty = TRUE,   seed = NA,   cluster = NULL,   file = NULL )

title: Constructor for a iglm Sampler — sampler.iglm
text: sampler_x object class `sampler.net.attr` (created `sampler.net.attr()`) specifying sample `x_attribute`. `NULL` (default), default `sampler.net.attr()` settings used. sampler_y object class `sampler.net.attr` specifying sample `y_attribute`. `NULL` (default), default settings used. sampler_z object class `sampler.net.attr` specifying sample `z_network` ties *within* defined neighborhood/overlap region. `NULL` (default), default settings used. n_simulation (integer) number independent samples generate burn-period. Default: 100. Must non-negative. n_burn_in (integer) number MCMC iterations discard start burn-. Default: 10. Must non-negative. init_empty (logical) `TRUE` (default), initialize MCMC chain empty state. seed (integer `NA`) single integer seed set sampling begins ensure reproducibility. `NA` (default), random seed generated automatically. cluster parallel cluster object (e.g., `parallel::makeCluster()`) parallel simulations. `NULL` (default), simulations run sequentially. file (character `NULL`) provided, loads sampler state specified .rds file instead initializing parameters.

title: Constructor for a iglm Sampler — sampler.iglm
text: object class `sampler.iglm` (`R6`).

title: Constructor for a iglm Sampler — sampler.iglm
code: n_actor <- 50 sampler_new <- sampler.iglm(   n_burn_in = 100, n_simulation = 10,   seed = 42,   sampler_x = sampler.net.attr(n_proposals = n_actor * 10),   sampler_y = sampler.net.attr(n_proposals = n_actor * 10),   sampler_z = sampler.net.attr(n_proposals = n_actor^2, tnt = TRUE),   init_empty = FALSE ) sampler_new #> Sampler settings #> ------------------------------------------------------------ #> Core parameters #>   n_simulation :10 #>   n_burn_in    :100 #>   init_empty   :FALSE #>   seed         :42 #>  #> Sub-samplers #>   sampler_x: #>     Number of proposals : 500 #>     TNT sampling        : TRUE #>   sampler_y: #>     Number of proposals : 500 #>     TNT sampling        : TRUE #>   sampler_z: #>     Number of proposals : 2500 #>     TNT sampling        : TRUE sampler_new$seed #> [1] 42 sampler_new$set_n_simulation(100) sampler_new$n_simulation #> [1] 100

what: Single Component Sampler Settings (R6 Class) — sampler.net.attr.generator
title: Single Component Sampler Settings (R6 Class) — sampler.net.attr.generator
text: `sampler_net_attr` class simple R6 container used within `sampler.iglm` class. holds MCMC sampling parameters single component `iglm` model, one attribute (e.g., `x_attribute`) part network (e.g., `z_network` within overlap). stores number proposals TNT flag. random seed managed centrally parent `sampler.iglm` object.

title: Single Component Sampler Settings (R6 Class) — sampler.net.attr.generator
text: n_proposals (`integer`) Read-. Number MCMC proposals per step. tnt (`logical`) Read-. Whether TNT sampling used.

text: Simulate responses connections.

title: Simulate Responses and Connections — simulate_iglm
code: simulate_iglm(   formula,   basis = NULL,   coef,   coef_degrees = NULL,   sampler = NULL,   only_stats = TRUE,   display_progress = FALSE,   offset_nonoverlap = 0,   cluster = NULL,   fix_x = FALSE,   fix_z = FALSE )

title: Simulate Responses and Connections — simulate_iglm
text: formula model `formula` object. left-hand side name `iglm.data` object available calling environment. See iglm-terms details specifying right-hand side terms. basis optional `iglm.data` object serve basis simulation. provided, simulation starts state defined object. `NULL` (default), initial state taken `iglm.data` object referenced `formula`. coef Numeric vector containing coefficient values structural (non-degrees) terms defined `formula`. coef_degrees Numeric vector specifying degrees coefficient values (expansiveness/attractiveness). required `formula` includes degrees terms. length must `n_actor` (undirected networks) `2 * n_actor` (directed networks), `n_actor` determined `iglm.data` object formula. sampler object class `sampler.iglm` (created `sampler.iglm()`) specifying MCMC sampling parameters. includes number simulations (`n_simulation`), burn-iterations (`n_burn_in`), initialization settings (`init_empty`), component sampler settings (`sampler_x`, `sampler_y`, etc.). `NULL` (default), default settings `sampler.iglm()` used. only_stats (logical). TRUE (default, consistent usage signature), function returns matrix features calculated simulation. full simulated iglm.data objects discarded minimize memory usage. FALSE, complete simulated iglm.data objects created returned within samples component output list. display_progress Logical. `TRUE`, progress messages progress bar (depending backend implementation) displayed simulation. Default `FALSE`. offset_nonoverlap Numeric scalar value passed C++ simulator. value typically added linear predictor dyads part 'overlap' set defined `iglm.data` object, potentially modifying tie probabilities outside primary neighborhood. Default `0`. cluster Optional parallel cluster object created, example, “parallel::makeCluster“. provided valid, function performs single burn-simulation main R process, distributes remaining `n_simulation` tasks across cluster workers using “parallel::parLapply“. master seed offset worker ensure different random streams. `NULL` (default), simulations run sequentially main R process. fix_x Logical. `TRUE`, simulation holds `x_attribute` fixed initial state (iglm.data object) simulates `y_attribute` `z_network`. `FALSE` (default), components (x, y, z) simulated according model sampler settings. fix_z Logical. `TRUE`, simulation holds `z_network` fixed initial state (iglm.data object). `FALSE` (default), network component simulated according model sampler settings.

title: Simulate Responses and Connections — simulate_iglm
text: list containing one two components (depending `only_stats`): `samples` `only_stats = FALSE`, list length     `sampler$n_simulation` element `iglm.data` object     representing one simulated draw model. list S3 class     `"iglm.data.list"`. `only_stats = TRUE`, component omitted. `stats` numeric matrix `sampler$n_simulation` rows     `length(coef)` columns. row contains features     (corresponding model terms `formula`) calculated one     simulation draw. Column names set match term names.

title: Simulate Responses and Connections — simulate_iglm
text: Parallel Execution: `cluster` object provided, simulation process adapted: single simulation run (including burn-specified `sampler$n_burn_in`)     performed master node obtain starting state parallel chains. total number requested simulations (`sampler$n_simulation`) divided     among cluster workers. “parallel::parLapply“ used run simulations worker.     worker starts state obtained initial burn-, performs     zero additional burn-(`n_burn_in = 0` passed workers), generates     assigned share simulations. Component sampler seeds offset     based worker ID ensure pseudo-independent random number streams. Results (simulated objects statistics) workers collected     combined. approach ensures initial burn-phase happens , saving time.

title: Simulate Responses and Connections — simulate_iglm
text: function stops error : length `coef` match number terms derived `formula`. `formula_preprocess` fails. `sampler` object class `sampler.iglm`. C++ backend `xyz_simulate_cpp` encounters error. Helper functions like `XYZ_to_R` `is_cluster_active` found. Warnings may issued default sampler settings used.

what: Twitter (X) data list for U.S. state legislators (10-state subset) — state_twitter
title: Twitter (X) data list for U.S. state legislators (10-state subset) — state_twitter
text: data object data derived Twitter (X) interactions U.S. state legislators, subset data analyzed Fritz et al. (2025).' data filtered include legislators 10 states (NY, CA, TX, FL, IL, PA, OH, GA, NC, MI) subset largest connected component based mention retweet activity. object contains main iglm.data object 5 pre-computed dyadic covariates.

title: Twitter (X) data list for U.S. state legislators (10-state subset) — state_twitter
Twitter (X) data list for U.S. state legislators (10-state subset) — state_twitter
text: list object containing 6 components. Let N number legislators filtered 10-state subset. iglm.data iglm.data object (also list)     parameterized follows: x_attribute: binary numeric vector length N.         Value 1 legislator's party 'Republican',         0 otherwise. y_attribute: Poisson numeric vector length N.         Represents count hatespeech incidents         (actors_data$number_hatespeech) legislator. z_network: directed edgelist (2-column matrix)         size n_edges x 2. tie (, j) exists legislator         either mentioned retweeted legislator j. neighborhood: directed edgelist (2-column matrix).         Represents follower network, tie (, j) exists         legislator follows legislator j.         Self-loops (diagonal) included.  match_gender N x N matrix. matrix[, j] = 1 legislator     legislator j gender, 0 otherwise. match_race N x N matrix. matrix[, j] = 1 legislator     legislator j race, 0 otherwise. match_state N x N matrix. matrix[, j] = 1 legislator     legislator j state, 0 otherwise. white_attribute 1 x N matrix (row vector). matrix[1, ] = 1     legislator 'White', 0 otherwise. gender_attribute 1 x N matrix (row vector). matrix[1, ] = 1     legislator 'female', 0 otherwise.

Legislative Studies Quarterly 47, 1023–1041. Fritz, C., Schweinberger, M. , Bhadra S., D. R. Hunter (2025). Regression Framework Studying Relationships among Attributes Network Interference. Journal American Statistical Association, appear.

text: formula model `formula` object. left-hand side name iglm.data object available calling environment. Alternatively, left-hand side can iglm.data.list object compute statistics multiple iglm.data objects (, e.g., normal outcome simulations). See iglm-terms details specifying right-hand side terms.

title: Compute Statistics — statistics
text: named numeric vector. element corresponds term   `formula`, value calculated observed feature   term based data iglm.data object. names   vector match coefficient names derived formula terms.

title: Compute Statistics — statistics
code: # Create a iglm.data object n_actor <- 10 neighborhood <- matrix(1, nrow = n_actor, ncol = n_actor) type_x <- "binomial" type_y <- "binomial" x_attr_data <- rbinom(n_actor, 1, 0.5) y_attr_data <- rbinom(n_actor, 1, 0.5) z_net_data <- matrix(0, nrow = n_actor, ncol = n_actor) object <- iglm.data(   z_network = z_net_data, x_attribute = x_attr_data,   y_attribute = y_attr_data, neighborhood = neighborhood,   directed = FALSE, type_x = type_x, type_y = type_y ) statistics(object ~ edges(mode = "local") + attribute_y + attribute_x) #> edges(mode = 'local')           attribute_y           attribute_x  #>                     0                     6                     6
